comment_text: The zero digit wouldn’t be a shortened strike-line because that would make for instance 2 and 12 identical. So it is either a lengthened line with no mark, or a small notch to indicate that the line was lengthened intentionally to show a zero-value in that segment.
The six day week is a hexday

The manor should have some kind of protective or alarming zan lattice on its back gateway that Kelyra disables with her magic by draining it, which is difficult and justifies Davian (to some extent) wanting to exclude her from the combat. In general, Zan technology should be more prevalent and certainly would be seen in Clade architecture on a routine basis. But even low class people could use rough etched Zan for light, alarms, etc.

It also requires more technical ability to etch the small details that make up ancient Zan, and the materials for doing so are more expensive. So even if someone else knew what Zirene did, they wouldn’t be able to replicate what she did necessarily, and it certainly wouldn’t be cheap. See Archeon’s attempted knockoffs with their own zanwright (who in a later book will help Kelyra make more Zan-tech for anti clade activities)

Writing style - try to be subtle when including world building stuff instead of beating the reader over the head with it, especially when it comes to moralistic statements etc - what would the character actually be thinking, as someone who’s used to the situation?

To Davian - did you really think that after all these years in the shadows you could come out with her into the light? - Kelyra isn’t very happy he wants to go help Maya after they kill Serathis. Sets Kelyra up as an antagonist in later books, wanting to kill Maya 

Maya would have done some admin work before presentation and been familiar with it, but now she is doing it more public-facing. Think transition from med student to resident 

Sorevin didn’t have any children who could Shroud, certainly none that could be Presented (at a Low Clade Presentation where high clades generally wouldn’t deign to attend except for the master of ceremonies). So when he dies Maya wants to bring someone from his house in that survived but Niall lets her know that’s not how it works, but they could join another proxy low Clade & Maya could designate that one as the new contract handlers

Niall to Maya after presentation- you have no idea of the political ramifications of what you did.
We really only need one export button in the comment export section, just pick whichever is the easiest since it won’t matter for the user.
Make the last export time easier for the user to read, right now it’s very messy. Clean it up and make the formatting clearer.
Is there a way to give everyone a username and password (hardcoded per person) and then automate sending comments? That would be better if people agree to be beta readers.

I wonder about keeping accounts on the drive or something. It’s mostly for persistence between devices and automation rather than security but some security would be nice. It would be a very limited number of accounts I manage myself.

I will have to think about sequencing the endgame fights. Right now it’s kind of Davian-heavy and Maya is not doing as much. If Davian comes into the throne room with Serathis and Niall, then he has to go free his mom and come back and Maya is still fighting Serathis, and then she leaves, Davian and Kelyra kill Serathis, and then he goes down and helps Maya and Niall. So what are some options? Davian goes off on his own to find his mom, somehow - maybe they get info from a spy or Aldira’s sources where she is specifically - and he splits off when they enter the Archeon citadel. However after Serathis triggers the trap for Niall it just leaves Maya fighting Serathis alone until Davian and Kelyra come. That’s an issue for a couple reasons, one of is that Maya should be able to beat Serathis. Serathis could summon a TON of her kids which is somewhat plausible but then why wouldn’t she have sent them to get Davian? Could be she didn’t realize how valuable Davian was until Kelyra came back? So she kept back a ton, and Maya kills a bunch of them until Davian comes back - which is why it’s just him fighting Serathis (with Kelyra) instead of the other Archeon people? But then those people are all dead which could be a future issue.

So here where it gives the extra “a necessity given her responsibilities” that’s an example of extra bloat that I do NOT want - organic worldbuilding would just be commenting that it allows for freedom of movement and letting the reader think about the implications. Or Maya testing her ability to move in it, showing instead of telling.
This is an example of some of the quotes I’d like to keep. In general in the back half of the book I am pretty happy with the dialogue I’ve written, at least in general.
Again, I like the dialogue here.
Formatting issue - sign language should be in italics. Also, I will be rewriting more of it to make it the awkward ASL-like version in terms of how it reads.

When writing the first (alpha) version of any chapter and finalizing that chapter, store a permanent record version of that initial version, the same as how the original versions of the older chapters are stored. Same thing when in rewrites - once a chapter has been rewritten then store a full permanent beta rewrite of that chapter. In the later rewrites, things can be a little looser although it may be helpful to be able to indicate certain parts that shouldn’t be changed at all without explicit permission

You should treat comments from me as more definitive. You should treat comments from others as highly speculative, more of a discussion point than a clear steering direction. Give the pros/cons etc of those comments.

The comments box should accurately capture at least what general part of the chapter the user is looking at when they make a comment, it doesn’t need to be line-perfect but at least “the scene where they fight in the Trial” or something needs to be associated with the comments to make them useful
When using sync comments from the comments pull-up dialogue, the button should show the same “syncing” behavior as on the comments page.

Drosin should be established as essentially a High Clade magus level - although Archeon relies on a stack and Drosin needs to use tools, that doesn't mean he isn't an extremely dangerous combatant and certainly the most dangerous person Davian has to actually have a full fight with throughout Book 1 (his things with Caius are brief and interrupted, and he doesn't fight Vorren directly himself, just blocks/supports for Maya and Niall). He ultimately will need Kelyra's help to defeat Drosin, even if the fight is somewhat fair overall
Kelyra is chained up and unable to help; it's ultimately her manifestation of a Shroud that lets her break free.
current_heading: Chapter 42 · Kelyra is present but not yet able to simply walk out or solve the fight.

comment_text: So the thing with Kelyra here is that she should be unable to help specifically because she's restrained and can't manifest a Shroud. It wouldn't make sense for her to just be hanging out no matter her level of injury. Also Serathis would have healed her up (we saw this earlier) so she is physically well
